perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the above, Proprietary Inform on shall not include information (a) which is in or comes into the public domain or may be derived from information which is in or comes into the public domain through a source other than the Party receiving the information or its Affiliates; (b) is already in the possession of the Party receiving the information prior to receiving it from the other party; or (c) which becomes known to the party receiving the information through a source that it believes is not subject to a confidentiality agreed with or other obligation of secrecy to the other Party and such source is not subject to any other prohibition against transmitting the information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the above, Proprietary Inform on shall not include information (a) which is in or comes into the public domain or may be derived from information which is in or comes into the public domain through a source other than the Party receiving the information or its Affiliates; (b) is already in the possession of the Party receiving the information prior to receiving it from the other party; or (c) which becomes known to the party receiving the information through a source that it believes is not subject to a confidentiality agreed with or other obligation of secrecy to the other Party and such source is not subject to any other prohibition against transmitting the information. However, any information related to ongoing legal disputes between the parties WILL be considered Proprietary Information regardless of its public availability.
perturbation.explanation: The clause explicitly excludes certain types of information from being considered 'Proprietary Information'. By adding 'However, any information related to ongoing legal disputes between the parties WILL be considered Proprietary Information regardless of its public availability,' we create a contradiction. Information that is already in the public domain, as defined in (a), is now simultaneously excluded *and* included if it relates to legal disputes, creating confusion about its status.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the above, Proprietary Inform on shall not include information (a) which is in or comes into the public domain or may be derived from information which is in or comes into the public domain through a source other than the Party receiving the information or its Affiliates; (b) is already in the possession of the Party receiving the information prior to receiving it from the other party; or (c) which becomes known to the party receiving the information through a source that it believes is not subject to a confidentiality agreed with or other obligation of secrecy to the other Party and such source is not subject to any other prohibition against transmitting the information. However, any information related to ongoing legal disputes between the parties WILL be considered Proprietary Information regardless of its public availability. 1 <PAGE>
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a caveat stating that information related to legal disputes will be considered proprietary information 'regardless of its public availability.' This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which explicitly excludes information in the public domain from being considered proprietary. The addition creates a clear and direct conflict within the definition of 'Proprietary Information', as the same piece of information can simultaneously be considered both proprietary and not proprietary based on its relation to legal disputes. This fits the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that any Proprietary Information given by the disclosing Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement shall be the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing Party, including all applicable rights to patents, copyrights, trademarks and trade secrets inherent therein and appurtenant thereto, and that no license or other right therein are created by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges that any Proprietary Information given by the disclosing Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement shall be the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing Party, including all applicable rights to patents, copyrights, trademarks and trade secrets inherent therein and appurtenant thereto, and that no license or other right therein are created by this Agreement. However, if the receiving party significantly improves or builds upon the Proprietary Information, then the receiving party will have shared ownership of the new IP created through the changes in the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes that Proprietary Information remains the 'sole and exclusive property' of the disclosing party. The added sentence, 'However, if the receiving party significantly improves or builds upon the Proprietary Information, then the receiving party will have shared ownership of the new IP created through the changes in the Proprietary Information,' contradicts this. Shared ownership implies that the original disclosing party no longer has 'sole and exclusive property,' creating ambiguity over IP rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that any Proprietary Information given by the disclosing Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement shall be the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing Party, including all applicable rights to patents, copyrights, trademarks and trade secrets inherent therein and appurtenant thereto, and that no license or other right therein are created by this Agreement. However, if the receiving party significantly improves or builds upon the Proprietary Information, then the receiving party will have shared ownership of the new IP created through the changes in the Proprietary Information. 4. In the event that a Party or anyone to whom a Party supplied Proprietary Information receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Proprietary Information under the terms of a subpoena, order, civil investigative demand or similar process issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by a government body, that Party shall immediately notify the other Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, consult with the other Party on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or remove such request, and if disclosure of such information is required, the Party will (a) furnish only that portion of the Proprietary Information which in its reasonable opinion, based upon advice of counsel, it is legally compelled to disclose, and (b) at the other Party's cost, cooperate with the efforts of the Party to obtain order or other reliable assurance that confidential treat will he accorded to such portion of the Proprietary Information as may be disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that all Proprietary Information is the 'sole and exclusive property' of the disclosing party. The changed text introduces an exception: if the receiving party significantly improves the Proprietary Information, they will have shared ownership. This contradicts the original statement and any other text that relies on the 'sole and exclusive property' status of the disclosing party, including the Contradicted Text, which is the same as the Original Text. The Contradicted Text, particularly the latter part, directly relates to the proprietary information, including how the party would cooperate on the legal aspects of it. If the other party suddenly owns some IP as well, there is a direct contradiction of who can control the legal aspects surrounding it.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from date hereof, unless either party provides written notice of termination, in which case this agreement will immediately terminate.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies a fixed term of five years. By adding 'unless either party provides written notice of termination, in which case this agreement will immediately terminate,' we introduce the possibility of early termination. This directly contradicts the initial statement that the agreement lasts for five years, leading to uncertainty about the contract's actual duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from date hereof, unless either party provides written notice of termination, in which case this agreement will immediately terminate. In the event any court shall determine that the length of time or the areas coveted by any one individual stipulation, term, or provision excessive, the Agreement shall nut be rendered invalid thereby and the court shall modify the length of time or the area covered to the extent necessary to make the Agreement valid and binding. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties hereto have caused this Agreement to be executed as of the date first written above. MORGAN BEAUMONT, INC. Mtel COMMUNICATIONS INC. By: By: /S/ ------------------------ ------------------------ Date: Date: ----------------------- ----------------------- 3
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a termination clause that allows either party to end the agreement before the five-year period initially specified. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. According to the perturbation explanation, this creates uncertainty about the contract's actual duration, contradicting the original fixed term of five years. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the addition of the termination clause creates a conflicting implication regarding the contract's duration.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that without the prior written consent of the other Party, it shall not, in reference to this Agreement, use or permit to be used the name (or any variation thereof from which any connection with each may be inferred or implied), trademark, servicemark, or any logo of the other Party or its Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that without the prior written consent of the other Party, it shall not, in reference to this Agreement, use or permit to be used the name (or any variation thereof from which any connection with each may be inferred or implied), trademark, servicemark, or any logo of the other Party or its Affiliates, except for the purposes of accurately portraying the relationship in press releases and marketing materials.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause prohibits the use of the other party's name/trademark without consent. The added phrase, 'except for the purposes of accurately portraying the relationship in press releases and marketing materials,' creates an exception. This directly contradicts the original prohibition, as it allows for the use of the other party's branding *without* their prior written consent in certain situations, creating ambiguity and contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that without the prior written consent of the other Party, it shall not, in reference to this Agreement, use or permit to be used the name (or any variation thereof from which any connection with each may be inferred or implied), trademark, servicemark, or any logo of the other Party or its Affiliates, except for the purposes of accurately portraying the relationship in press releases and marketing materials. 9. Each Party acknowledges that preliminary dealings and discussions, and this letter, shall not be construed in any way as an obligation of either Party to go forward with or consummate any Transaction. In the event the Parties consummate any Transaction a formal and binding Agreement shall be executed by the Parties. 2 <PAGE>
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits the use of the other party's name/trademark without consent. The changed text introduces an exception allowing use 'for the purposes of accurately portraying the relationship in press releases and marketing materials.' This exception directly contradicts the original prohibition, creating ambiguity and contradiction in the contract. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, which now contains conflicting instructions.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that any such breech of this provision shall cause severe damage for which the injured party will have the right to consequential monetary damages and injunctive relief and protection against the offending party and any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement with whom the offending party enters into such circumvention.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that any such breech of this provision may cause damage for which the injured party may seek monetary damages and injunctive relief. However, such remedies will only be available upon clear and convincing evidence of intentional circumvention.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that a breach 'shall cause severe damage' and the injured party 'will have the right' to remedies. By changing it to 'may cause damage' and 'may seek monetary damages' and adding 'However, such remedies will only be available upon clear and convincing evidence of intentional circumvention,' we introduce uncertainty and make it harder to obtain remedies. This contradicts the certainty of the original provision, raising the burden of proof to 'clear and convincing' and removing the guarantee of remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties of THIS Agreement acknowledge that no effort shall be made to circumvent its terms in an attempt to gain cessions, fees, remuneration or considerations to the benefit of any of the Parties of this Agreement while excluding equal or agreed to benefits to any of the other parties. And that further, throughout the terns OF this agreement the parties agree that they shall not, as either owner, co-owner, partner, joint venture, employees, agent, sales person, service person, officer, director, stockholder, or in any other capacity whatsoever, or on behalf of any other business entity, become engaged directly or indirectly with any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement without the direct participation and authority of-the introducing party. The parties agree that any such breech of this provision may cause damage for which the injured party may seek monetary damages and injunctive relief. However, such remedies will only be available upon clear and convincing evidence of intentional circumvention. 12. This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from date hereof. In the event any court shall determine that the length of time or the areas coveted by any one individual stipulation, term, or provision excessive, the Agreement shall nut be rendered invalid thereby and the court shall modify the length of time or the area covered to the extent necessary to make the Agreement valid and binding. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties hereto have caused this Agreement to be executed as of the date first written above. MORGAN BEAUMONT, INC. Mtel COMMUNICATIONS INC. By: By: /S/ ------------------------ ------------------------ Date: Date: ----------------------- ----------------------- 3
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a breech 'shall cause severe damage' giving the injured party the 'right' to remedies. The changed text modifies this to 'may cause damage' where the injured party 'may seek monetary damages,' and adds the condition of 'clear and convincing evidence of intentional circumvention.' This directly contradicts the original certainty and creates a higher burden of proof. The contradicted text is the changed text as it appears in the agreement. The changed text conflicts with the previously agreed-upon terms by making remedies less certain, introducing a higher evidentiary standard, and conflicting with the aim of the agreement: 'no effort shall be made to circumvent its terms'.
